project: Amigos do Trabalho - Desktop Virtual Pet
description: Upgrade the desktop pet to fullscreen transparent companion with real GDExtension input, physics (gravity, drag-and-drop), idle walking AI, and a coin-purchasable furniture system with walkable surfaces and pet interactions.

userStories.title: Replace mock GlobalInput with real GDExtension
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the game to use the compiled GlobalInput C++ extension so that real keyboard and mouse events drive the coin system instead of simulated random input.
acceptanceCriteria: GlobalInput autoload entry removed from project.godot [autoload] section
global_input.gdextension is present in project root and referenced via Godot's extension system
coin_system.gd works unchanged — GlobalInput.get_key_count(), get_click_count(), reset_counts() resolve to the C++ singleton
scripts/mock_global_input.gd is deleted
main.gd calls GlobalInput.start_hooks() in _ready()
main.gd calls GlobalInput.stop_hooks() in _notification(NOTIFICATION_WM_CLOSE_REQUEST) before quit
No GDScript errors from GlobalInput references when parsing scripts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The GDExtension registers GlobalInput as a singleton via register_types.cpp Engine::register_singleton. Removing the autoload is sufficient — the C++ singleton takes over. The .so/.dll must be pre-compiled in gdextension/bin/.

userStories.title: Fullscreen transparent always-on-top window
userStories.description: As a user, I want the game window to cover my entire screen transparently so the pet and furniture appear to live on my desktop.
acceptanceCriteria: Window size set to DisplayServer.screen_get_size() at startup in main.gd _ready()
Window position set to Vector2.ZERO at startup
Window remains borderless, always-on-top, and transparent
project.godot display settings updated for fullscreen transparent behavior
Existing _update_passthrough() logic updated to dynamically cover all visible elements
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current window is a fixed small size. This changes it to cover the full primary monitor. Passthrough polygon must be rebuilt to include pet rect plus any future furniture/UI rects.

userStories.title: Pet state machine foundation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pet.gd to use a state machine (IDLE, WALKING, FALLING, DRAGGED, INTERACTING) so that movement behaviors are cleanly separated.
acceptanceCriteria: pet.gd has an enum PetState with values: IDLE, WALKING, FALLING, DRAGGED, INTERACTING
A current_state variable tracks the active state
_process() delegates to state-specific handler methods (e.g. _process_idle, _process_walking, etc.)
Existing click/interaction logic integrated into the state machine without breaking current behavior
Pet visual states (SAD/NEUTRAL/HAPPY) remain independent of movement states
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This refactors pet.gd to support the new movement features (drag, fall, walk) added in subsequent stories. Keep existing modulate color tinting and interaction menu logic working.

userStories.title: Pet drag-and-drop with gravity
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click and drag the pet and have it fall realistically when I release it.
acceptanceCriteria: Left-click + hold on the pet sprite enters DRAGGED state; pet follows the cursor
On release, pet enters FALLING state with configurable gravity (@export var gravity: float)
Pet lands on screen bottom (screen_height - pet_height) and transitions to IDLE
Pet does not fall below the screen bottom
While dragging, pet shows a grabbed visual (slight rotation or scale change)
Dragging does not open the interaction menu (menu opens only on click without drag — use 4px threshold)
Physics uses manual velocity integration (position += velocity * delta), not Godot RigidBody
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FR-6: Drag detection uses InputEventMouseButton press. If mouse moves more than 4px before release, it's a drag not a click. Convert pet from simple Sprite2D positioning to velocity-based movement.

userStories.title: Pet idle walking
userStories.description: As a user, I want the pet to walk around on its own when idle so it feels alive.
acceptanceCriteria: When IDLE, pet picks a random horizontal direction and distance (50-300px) after a random wait (1-5 seconds)
Pet walks at configurable speed (@export var walk_speed: float)
After reaching target, pet returns to IDLE and waits before next walk
Pet sprite flips horizontally based on walking direction
Pet is constrained to screen bounds (does not walk off screen edges)
Walking state is interrupted immediately when user starts dragging
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses the state machine from US-003. IDLE state has a timer; when it fires, transition to WALKING. WALKING moves toward target, then back to IDLE. Screen bottom is the only surface for now — furniture surfaces come in US-010.

userStories.title: FurnitureData resource class
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a structured FurnitureData resource so each piece of furniture has consistent, configurable properties.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/furniture_data.gd is a Resource class with exported fields: id (String), display_name (String), texture (Texture2D), coin_cost (int), walkable (bool), walk_surface_y_offset (int), can_fall_off_edge (bool), interaction_type (String), interaction_coin_bonus (int), interaction_cooldown (float)

At least 5 FurnitureData .tres files created under furniture/data/: sofa (walkable, sleep), bookshelf (not walkable, inert), food_bowl (not walkable, eat), toy (not walkable, play), table (walkable, no interaction)
Placeholder textures used (colored rectangles or existing icon)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are pure data resources. No scene or behavior logic yet. The .tres files can use simple placeholder textures — real art is not required for first iteration.

userStories.title: Furniture node scene
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable Furniture scene so each furniture instance in the world behaves consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: scenes/furniture.tscn with script scripts/furniture.gd extending Node2D
Takes @export var data: FurnitureData resource
Displays furniture texture via Sprite2D child
Exposes get_surface_y() returning global Y of walkable surface (top of sprite + walk_surface_y_offset)
Exposes get_left_x() and get_right_x() for edge detection
Has Area2D + CollisionShape2D child sized to the sprite for overlap detection
Emits pet_interacted(furniture) signal when triggered
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the runtime scene that gets instantiated for each placed furniture piece. The Area2D is used for pet proximity detection in US-012.

userStories.title: Furniture shop UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse and buy furniture with my coins so I can decorate my desktop.
acceptanceCriteria: A Shop button is added to the screen, anchored to a corner, always visible
Clicking Shop opens a panel listing all FurnitureData items with icon, name, and cost
Owned items show 'Owned', affordable items show 'Buy', too-expensive items are greyed out
Buying deducts coins via CoinSystem.spend_coins() and adds item to owned list
Owned furniture list saved to user://save_data.json alongside existing coins data
Shop panel included in passthrough polygon while visible
Shop closes when clicking outside it
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extend save_data.json schema to include owned_furniture array. Load FurnitureData resources from res://furniture/data/ at runtime into a registry dictionary.

userStories.title: Furniture placement in the world
userStories.description: As a user, I want owned furniture to appear on screen so my desktop looks decorated.
acceptanceCriteria: Owned furniture items are spawned into main scene at startup from save data
Each furniture piece has a saved position (Vector2) in save_data.json
Default positions assigned on first purchase (staggered along screen bottom)
Passthrough polygon updated to include all furniture rects
Furniture does not overlap the pet's starting position
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This loads the furniture scene (US-007) and positions instances based on save data. Positions are fixed after placement — no drag-to-reposition (non-goal).

userStories.title: Pet walks on furniture surfaces
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the pet to land on and walk along walkable furniture surfaces instead of always falling to the screen bottom.
acceptanceCriteria: When falling, pet lands on the first walkable furniture surface below it (comparing Y and X range)
Pet can also land on screen bottom if no furniture is below
When walking on a furniture surface, pet is constrained to that surface's left/right edges
If can_fall_off_edge is true for the furniture, pet walks off the edge and falls
If can_fall_off_edge is false, pet treats the edge as a wall and turns around
Non-walkable furniture is treated as a wall (pet does not walk through it)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This integrates furniture awareness into the pet's FALLING and WALKING states from US-004 and US-005. Surface detection: compare pet bottom Y against each walkable furniture's get_surface_y() within get_left_x()..get_right_x() range.

userStories.title: Pet interacts with interactive furniture
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the pet react when it walks into interactive furniture so the world feels dynamic.
acceptanceCriteria: When pet enters Area2D of interactive furniture (interaction_type != ''), pet enters INTERACTING state
Player earns interaction_coin_bonus coins via CoinSystem.add_coins()
Interaction blocked if interaction_cooldown has not elapsed since last trigger for that piece
sleep interaction: pet shows sleeping visual (e.g. Zzz label or tint) for 2-3 seconds then resumes
eat interaction: pet plays scale bounce animation and awards coins
play interaction: same bounce as existing _play_bounce() in pet.gd
Cooldown state resets on app restart (not persisted)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses the Furniture scene's pet_interacted signal and Area2D overlap detection. The INTERACTING state in the pet state machine blocks other movement until the interaction animation finishes.
